title: FY2025 Independent Audit Results -- Clean Opinion, $710M Retiree Health Liability, and $20M Budgetary Surplus
summary: Bonadio Group presents FY2025 independent audit results to Finance Committee. Clean/unmodified opinion on 114-page financial statements (900 audit hours). One material weakness (capital projects tracking -- repeat finding) and three significant deficiencies identified. GASB 101 (compensated absences) implemented. Audit delayed ~1 month from planned January 31 issuance due to SEFA (Schedule of Federal Awards) going back and forth through February. Bureau of Accounts (BOA) currently down one staff position, raising concerns for future audit timelines. Key financial highlights: $710M unrestricted deficit driven entirely by retiree health liability (state provides no mechanism to pre-fund); general fund budgeted to use $19M of fund balance but actually increased by $1.7M, producing ~$20M budgetary surplus; unfilled positions identified as key driver of expenditure savings; school district similarly had $27M surplus; water fund profit $3.7M, sewer $2M. Unassigned fund balance at $114M -- approximately 50% of annual expenditures, historically unprecedented. Audit difficulty rated 'moderate' relative to other municipalities.

Finance Committee Chair not identified by name in transcript
Bonadio Group = external CPA firm specializing in municipal audits (counties, cities, towns, villages, school districts)
BOA = Bureau of Accounts (city finance staff); currently understaffed
SEFA = Schedule of Expenditures of Federal Awards; delayed completion caused audit delay
